generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance MotorVoltageConverter : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electronic Speed Controller (Vedder) that converts desired speed commands into motor voltage commands via linear mapping for the autonomous vehicle's motor actuation system. Adheres to ISO 15622 Performance Class I ACC standards for speed regulation."
]

instance SteeringServoActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Servo mechanism that adjusts the autonomous vehicle's steering direction based on control signals from the Digital Twin System's ACC logic. Interfaced with the Physical Twin via ROS2 nodes."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "3D LiDAR sensor (Hokuyo UST-10LX) generating point cloud data for object detection and environmental perception in the Physical Twin autonomous vehicle system."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HokuyoPointCloudData, HokuyoObjectDetectionData
]

instance IntelDeepsenseD435iCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Depth camera (Intel Deepsense D435i) providing supplementary visual data for object tracking and environmental context in the Physical Twin autonomous vehicle system."
    DTDFVocab:producedData DepthCameraVisualData
]

instance VehicleSpeedSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Vehicle speed sensor measuring the autonomous vehicle's instantaneous speed in meters per second for ego vehicle state monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleSpeedData
]

instance SteeringAngleSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Steering angle sensor measuring the autonomous vehicle's current steering angle in degrees for ego vehicle state monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringAngleData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance HokuyoPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw LiDAR point cloud data from Hokuyo UST-10LX, pre-processed via DBSCAN clustering and Kalman filtering for object state estimation. Transmitted via ROS2 IPC with ~10-30 Hz frequency."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
]

instance HokuyoObjectDetectionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Object detection data derived from Hokuyo LiDAR point clouds, containing detected objects with position, velocity, and size information. Transmitted via ROS2 IPC with ~10-30 Hz frequency."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
]

instance DepthCameraVisualData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Visual data from Intel Deepsense D435i depth camera, used as supplementary information for object tracking. Transmitted via ROS2 IPC with sensor-dependent frequency."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelDeepsenseD435iCamera
]

instance VehicleSpeedData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time vehicle speed data in meters per second, transmitted from Physical Twin to Digital Twin Entity via ROS2 IPC with 100 Hz frequency."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleSpeedSensor
]

instance SteeringAngleData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time steering angle data in degrees, transmitted from Physical Twin to Digital Twin Entity via ROS2 IPC with 100 Hz frequency."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringAngleSensor
]

instance ObjectTrackDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Serialized object track data containing (ID, position, velocity, size) tuples updated at perception cycle frequency (~10-30 Hz). Transmitted via Wi-Fi UDP/TCP protocols through Data Translation Functional Entity."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR, IntelDeepsenseD435iCamera
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance DBSCANClusteringModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "DBSCAN clustering algorithm implementation for segmenting LiDAR point clouds into meaningful object clusters within the Physical Twin's Data Pre-Processing Functional Entity."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudProcessingEnabler
]

instance KalmanFilterModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Kalman filter implementation for object state estimation and trajectory prediction using data from LiDAR point clouds and depth camera visual data."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ObjectTrackingEnabler
]

instance ActuationMappingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Linear mapping model converting desired speed commands from Digital Twin Entity into motor voltage commands for the Physical Twin's actuation system."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ActuationControlEnabler
]

instance ObjectTrackData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured object track data containing (ID, position, velocity, size) tuples updated at perception cycle frequency (~10-30 Hz). Stored in memory for real-time processing and reporting."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ObjectTrackDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ObjectTrackingEnabler, DataVisualizationEnabler
]

instance EgoVehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Vehicle state data including speed (m/s) and steering angle (degrees) stored in SQL database for historical reporting and analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleSpeedData, SteeringAngleData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ReportingEnabler, DataVisualizationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance PointCloudProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2-based Data Pre-Processing Functional Entity implementing DBSCAN clustering and initial point cloud segmentation for LiDAR data."
    DTDFVocab:enables ObjectDetectionService, ObjectTrackingService
]

instance ObjectTrackingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2-based Functional Entity implementing Kalman filtering for object state estimation and trajectory prediction using processed LiDAR and depth camera data."
    DTDFVocab:enables ObjectTrackingService, ACCControlService
]

instance ActuationControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2-based Functional Entity implementing the actuation mapping model that converts Digital Twin control commands into physical actuation signals for the autonomous vehicle."
    DTDFVocab:enables ActuationService
]

instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Cross-System Functional Entity managing Wi-Fi-based UDP/TCP data translation between Physical Twin Entity and Digital Twin Entity with 100 ms end-to-end latency requirement."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance ROS2NetworkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2 inter-process communication middleware enabling real-time data exchange between all Functional Entities within the Physical Twin and Digital Twin Systems."
    DTDFVocab:enables PointCloudProcessingEnabler, ObjectTrackingEnabler, DataVisualizationEnabler
]

instance SQLDatabaseEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SQL database system storing historical vehicle state data and object track information for reporting and analysis purposes."
    DTDFVocab:enables ReportingService
]

instance ACCSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2-based simulation environment offloading Adaptive Cruise Control logic execution from the resource-constrained Physical Twin ECU to the Digital Twin Entity."
    DTDFVocab:enables ACCControlService
]

// C6: Services
instance ObjectDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service detecting and tracking objects in the vehicle's proximity using processed LiDAR point clouds and depth camera data."
    DTDFVocab:provides ObjectDetectionInsight
]

instance ObjectTrackingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service maintaining object state estimates and trajectories using Kalman filtering on processed sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:provides ObjectTrackingInsight
]

instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing real-time visualization of ego vehicle state (speed, steering) and object tracks through a 3D visualization user interface."
    DTDFVocab:provides VehicleStateMonitoringInsight, ObjectTrackVisualizationInsight
]

instance ACCControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service implementing Adaptive Cruise Control logic including lead vehicle detection, speed regulation, and trajectory planning executed on the Digital Twin Entity."
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCControlAction, SpeedAdjustmentAction
]

instance ActuationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service converting Digital Twin control commands into physical actuation signals for the autonomous vehicle's motor and steering systems."
    DTDFVocab:provides MotorVoltageCommandAction, SteeringCommandAction
]

instance ReportingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service generating historical reports and analytics from stored vehicle state and object track data in the SQL database."
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceReportInsight, DebuggingDataInsight
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling user-controlled ACC operations including enable/disable, desired speed adjustment, and emergency braking through a control panel interface."
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCActivationAction, EmergencyBrakingAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance ObjectDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing detected objects' positions, velocities, and sizes in the vehicle's proximity for situational awareness."
]

instance ObjectTrackingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight showing object state estimates and predicted trajectories for collision avoidance and path planning."
]

instance VehicleStateMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time insight showing the autonomous vehicle's current speed and steering angle for driver/operator awareness."
]

instance ObjectTrackVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "3D visualization of object tracks and projected driving path for operational monitoring and debugging."
]

instance ACCControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic action performing lead vehicle detection and speed regulation according to ACC logic."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SpeedAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic action adjusting the vehicle's speed based on lead vehicle dynamics and operator input."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance MotorVoltageCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic action sending motor voltage commands to the Physical Twin's actuation system."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SteeringCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic action sending steering angle commands to the Physical Twin's steering system."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ACCActivationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "User-controlled action enabling or disabling the Adaptive Cruise Control system."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EmergencyBrakingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "User-controlled action triggering immediate emergency braking of the autonomous vehicle."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance PerformanceReportInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Historical insight providing performance metrics and analytics from stored vehicle state and object track data."
]

instance DebuggingDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight containing raw and processed data for debugging purposes, including object track anomalies and system performance metrics."
]

// C18: Functional Entities
instance PhysicalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Functional Entity hosting resource-specific functionality including sensors, actuators, and pre-processing modules for the autonomous vehicle's Physical Twin."
    base:contains HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR, IntelDeepsenseD435iCamera, VehicleSpeedSensor, SteeringAngleSensor,
                 MotorVoltageConverter, SteeringServoActuator,
                 PointCloudProcessingEnabler, ObjectTrackingEnabler
]

instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Functional Entity executing control logic, data collection, and reporting services as a surrogate for the Physical Twin's ECU during development and testing."
    base:contains ACCSimulationEnabler, DataTranslationEnabler, SQLDatabaseEnabler,
                 DataVisualizationEnabler, ReportingEnabler,
                 ACCControlEnabler, ActuationControlEnabler
]

instance UserEntity : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Functional Entity providing user interfaces for monitoring, control, and reporting services."
    base:contains PresentationEnabler, UserInterfaceEnabler
]

instance CrossSystemEntity : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Functional Entity managing data translation between Physical Twin Entity and Digital Twin Entity via Wi-Fi communication protocols."
    base:contains DataTranslationEnabler
]

// C19: Enablers (continued)
instance PresentationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "3D Visualization User Interface Functional Entity providing real-time visualization of vehicle state and object tracks."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance UserInterfaceEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Control Panel User Interface Functional Entity enabling remote management of ACC operations."
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]

instance DataVisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler providing 3D visualization of object tracks, vehicle state, and projected driving path for operational monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ObjectTrackData, EgoVehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance ReportingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler generating historical reports and analytics from stored vehicle state and object track data."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EgoVehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:enables ReportingService
]

instance ACCControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2-based Functional Entity implementing the Adaptive Cruise Control logic executed on the Digital Twin Entity."
    DTDFVocab:enables ACCControlService
]

// C18: Functional Entities (continued)
instance SystemUnderStudy : DTDFVocab:SystemUnderStudy [
    base:desc "1/10-scale autonomous vehicle serving as the Physical Twin for an Adaptive Cruise Control Digital Twin system."
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment PhysicalTwinEntity, DigitalTwinEntity, UserEntity, CrossSystemEntity
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The virtual-to-physical interaction includes:
  - **Control commands**: ACC-derived desired speed adjustments (Hz) and emergency braking signals sent to the PT’s **Actuation Functional Entity** via **ROS2 nodes**.
  - **Actuation signals**: Motor voltage commands (converted from desired speed) and steering servo adjustments.
  Commands are transmitted via **Wi-Fi network** (Data Translation Functional Entity) to the PT’s **Actuation Functional Entity**, which executes them on the **Vedder Electronic Speed Controller** and **steering servo**. The system ensures **clock synchronization (±100 ms)** between PT and DTS for coordinated actuation."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin operates on a **real-time time scale** with the following specifications:
  - **End-to-end latency** between the vehicle and Digital Twin Entity is maintained at **≤100 ms**, compliant with **3GPP Technical Specification 22.185** for Vehicle-to-Vehicle (V2V) communication.
  - **Clock synchronization** between components is accurate to **±100 ms**, achieved via distributed system protocols (e.g., **ROS2 time synchronization**).
  - **Data collection and processing** occur at **perception cycle frequencies** (e.g., **LiDAR data** at ~10 Hz, **camera data** at ~30 Hz), with **object tracking updates** at **~10 Hz** (Kalman filter rate).
  - **ACC control loop** operates at a **fixed update rate** (not explicitly stated but inferred to align with vehicle dynamics, typically **10–100 Hz** for real-time systems)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin constellation is structured as a **multi-entity, cross-layer architecture** with the following orchestration:
  1) **Physical Twin Entity**: Hosts sensors (LiDAR, camera), actuators (motor/servo), and pre-processing (DBSCAN + Kalman filter).
  2) **Digital Twin Entity**: Executes ACC logic (Controlling FE), collects data (Data Collecting FE), and generates reports (Reporting FE). Split into **real-time** (timesensitive computations) and **offline** components (e.g., data analysis).
  3) **User Entity**: Provides **3D Visualization** (Presentation FE) and **Control Panel** (User Interface FE) via a unified UI.
  4) **Cross-System Entity**: Manages **Data Translation** (serialization, unit conversion, message alignment) between entities.
  The constellation adheres to **ISO 23247 entity-based RA** but introduces a **Physical Twin Entity** to encapsulate PT-specific resources. Communication flows via **ROS2 (intra-entity)** and **Wi-Fi (inter-entity)**. The system is designed for **Human-in-the-Loop (Liberté to act vs. Ability to act taxonomy)** with operators/developers as primary users."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin supports **operational and developmental lifecycle stages**:
  - **Design/Development**: Used as a **cyber-physical experimentation facility** for ACC logic validation and parameter tuning (e.g., Kalman filter calibration, DBSCAN thresholds).
  - **Operation**: Enables **real-time monitoring, remote management, and predictive maintenance** of the ACC system during vehicle operation.
  The DT represents the **as-operated state** of the physical twin, with models validated against real-world sensor data (e.g., LiDAR point clouds, object tracks). No explicit 'as-manufactured' or 'as-designed' representations are mentioned in the documents."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
  - **Sensor Data Fidelity**: LiDAR/camera data validated against real-world object detection (e.g., object tracks updated at perception cycle frequency).
  - **Model Accuracy**: **Kalman filter** and **DBSCAN** parameters tuned via empirical testing on the PT.
  - **Latency Validation**: End-to-end latency tested against **3GPP TS 22.185** (<100 ms for V2V communication).
  - **Clock Synchronization**: Verified via **ROS2 clock synchronization** (±100 ms accuracy).
  - **ACC Compliance**: Validated against **ISO 15622 Performance Class I** standards for speed regulation.
  Uncertainty sources include **sensor noise** (LiDAR/camera) and **network jitter** (Wi-Fi communication), mitigated via **real-time filtering** and **hardware-based clock synchronization**."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System employs the following technical connections:
  - **Intra-Entity Communication**: **ROS2 inter-process communication (IPC)** for intra-entity messaging (e.g., PT Entity → DTE).
  - **Inter-Entity Communication**: **Wi-Fi-based UDP/TCP** (Data Translation Functional Entity) for cross-entity data exchange (e.g., PT → DTE, DTE → PT).
  - **Network Protocols**: **ROS2 serialization** (for intra-entity) and **custom message formats** (for inter-entity, aligned via Data Translation FE).
  - **Latency Requirements**: **<100 ms end-to-end** (PT-to-DT-to-PT) as per **3GPP TS 22.185** for V2V communication.
  - **Clock Synchronization**: **ROS2 clock synchronization** (±100 ms) for temporal alignment of PT and DTS components."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System is deployed in a **distributed, hardware-agnostic architecture**:
  - **Physical Twin Entity**: Hosted on the **1/10-scale autonomous vehicle** (Jetson Orin Nano, ROS2).
  - **Digital Twin Entity**: Potentially hosted on **edge/cloud infrastructure** (not specified in documents) for offloading compute-intensive tasks (e.g., ACC logic).
  - **User Entity**: Hosted on a **remote workstation** (3D visualization, control panel).
  - **Data Storage**: External **SQL database** (off-vehicle) for historical data reporting.
  The system leverages **ROS2’s hardware abstraction** for portability across deployment environments. No explicit mention of **containerization** (e.g., Docker) or **orchestration tools** (e.g., Kubernetes) in the documents."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System integrates horizontally with:
  - **External Sensors/Actuators**: Not explicitly mentioned, but the system is designed for **modular expansion** via ROS2 (e.g., additional LiDAR/camera nodes).
  - **Simulation Tools**: Leverages **ROS2-based simulation** for ACC logic offloading (e.g., surrogate ECU testing). No explicit integration with third-party simulators (e.g., CREO Simulate) in this case study.
  - **Cloud Services**: Potential offloading of **Digital Twin Entity** components to cloud infrastructure (not implemented here but implied for scalability).
  - **Data Exchange Standards**: Uses **custom message formats** (serialized via ROS2/Data Translation FE) for interoperability. No mention of **OPC UA** or **DDS** in the documents."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "Data ownership and privacy considerations in the documents are limited to:
  - **Data Storage**: Ego vehicle state and object track data stored in an **external SQL database** (owned by the DTS developers/operators).
  - **Access Control**: No explicit mention of **role-based access control (RBAC)** or **encryption** for data in transit/rest. The system assumes **trusted network** for Wi-Fi communication.
  - **Ethical Implications**: Not addressed in the documents. The PT is a **1/10-scale autonomous vehicle** (no human passengers), reducing safety/privacy risks. No discussion on **GDPR compliance** or **anonymization** of sensor data (e.g., object tracks)."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System adheres to the following standards:
  - **ISO 23247**: Reference architecture (Part 2) for **entity-based DT design**, with deviations (e.g., Physical Twin Entity) to accommodate automotive needs.
  - **ISO 15622**: **Performance Class I ACC standards** for speed regulation logic.
  - **3GPP TS 22.185**: **Latency requirements** (<100 ms) for V2V communication.
  - **IEC 62541 (OPC UA)**: Not implemented but referenced as a potential alternative for **Data Translation Functional Entity** communication.
  - **ROS2**: De facto standard for **robotics/middleware** in the automotive domain.
  The system also follows **automotive best practices** (e.g., modularity, loose coupling) for system design."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety considerations include:
  - **Safety**:
    - **Real-time constraints**: Enforced via **<100 ms latency** and **clock synchronization (±100 ms)** to prevent timing-related failures.
    - **ACC compliance**: Validated against **ISO 15622 Performance Class I** for safe speed regulation.
    - **Emergency braking**: Implemented via **remote user commands** (Control Panel UI) with direct actuation on the PT.
    - **Human-in-the-Loop**: Operators/developers retain control via **remote management** (e.g., ACC enable/disable).
  - **Security**:
    - **Network Security**: Assumes **trusted Wi-Fi network** for inter-entity communication (no mention of encryption or firewalls).
    - **Data Integrity**: Ensured via **ROS2 clock synchronization** and **time-stamped messaging** (Data Translation FE).
    - **Access Control**: Not explicitly addressed; UI-based commands (e.g., emergency braking) require **user authentication** (implied but not detailed).
    - **Safety-Critical Operations**: Remote actuation (e.g., braking) is **operator-initiated**, reducing risks of unauthorized control. No discussion on **cyber-physical attack mitigation** (e.g., adversarial input validation)."
]
